better now?

Okay.
So welcome to the team.
Thank you so much.
It's been only day two when I feel as if I've gone through a lot of context in regards to growthx and what the company stands for and all of those things.
Thank
Yeah, that's why I wanted to reach out to you to let you know that you feel free to reach out to ask questions.
When I started, it was five months ago.
It was really hard to understand how the tools interacted.
So I wish I had someone to ask a lot of questions so I can be that person for you.
So when you, we can set another meeting for us to run an article.
You will be working with one client that is called Immubit.

And I think, I don't know how, what type of learner you are, but for me, was really important to actually start implementing things like doing actual work that helped me understand things better and the tools and everything else.
And I think like for this client, Immubit, so you can start reading about the client.

Reading everything we have, the kickoff happened last week, so we are starting, that's a very good thing for you, because we will be starting together with the clients, and we will give us, like you're going to be able to educate yourself, and I'll be doing the same process, while we build the strategy and the first articles.

So just to give you a brief description of this client, they are an AI solution that optimizes energy consumption in big factory plants, so like super complex, because their keywords are niched, so that gives us more opportunities to, because for other clients, there's a lot of competition.
For keywords that are high volume, but very high keyword div.
We'll see.
So I'll give you the document where you can study what we have about the client.
And maybe if you want, I don't want to overwhelm you too much.

I want to respect the time you need to go through the onboarding, but I also want to let you know that if in case you feel that will be easier for you to start working on some assignments, we can have that in the next time.
That definitely helps a lot.
And I think there's also, at least Bridget mentioned that within this particular onboarding cohort, they're already giving like a demo assignment for us to do.
So I think working on an actual client project would also be really, really helpful.
It'll give me, like I'll be able to dip my toes into the water before I'm, you know, asked to handle like three clients or four clients or whatever it is.
So that definitely.
I'm glad they have that now.
Yes, didn't know what I supposed to do with the workflows.
I had the logins for the platforms, for AirTable, but I didn't know how was the interaction between platforms and everything else.
But it's good that we will get to build everything for this client from zero, so that's a good opportunity.
And if you have any sort of questions about how things work and everything else, feel free to reach out.
Yeah, absolutely.
At the moment, I just have like a few, or will say benign questions.
So, in one of the onboarding tasks, where it's at starting in your account, it says that I need access to E3D Ops delivery and onboarding team.
Sorry, can you repeat that?
It says that in the linear account, I need access to ETD delivery and onboarding teams.
I'm not sure if you're the correct person to ask, but if I should just ask Bridget regarding what this entails.
Are we talking about Slack channel?
Are we talking about some sort of specific tools?
Because I know that there is one tool that's scheduled on Friday this week.
And after that, I guess we'll get our logins and whatnot.
But at the moment, is there any channel or any pod that I should be aware of?
I remember when I started, we had like a Notion page with a checklist of all the systems needed access for.
Okay.
there was a lot of And I know that Vietz, that's W-E-I-E-S.
And he's the person who can get us access to every tool.
wonderful.
cool.
And I
And you have an onboarding doc, right?
With these instructions, no?
You don't have any onboarding doc?
We have an onboarding doc.

And there are like a bunch of tasks assigned there already.
Yesterday, I got through all the tasks that were planned.
And I was just actually looking at tasks that were planned for later in the week, just to see what was up and what I should anticipate.
Okay.
If you have any questions about the onboarding docs, you can ask Bridget, but probably about the having access to the systems.
Vits is the person who will help me a lot when I started.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
I asked you about your schedule, your work schedule.
I added a note to my wall, not to bother you.
Okay.
Thank
So in weird working hours for you, but I don't know if you have that.
You said that you're going to be adding to your calendar, right?
Yes.

So if I send you a Slack message, I don't know if it's possible to set, to have, don't send notifications outside your working hours, because usually I like to send the message, and if you can't, I actually need, that's totally okay.
And the same for me, like if want to, if you're working in your hours and you want to send me something not to forget or something like that, go ahead and send it.
If I'm not able to read, I won't read or do anything about it, okay?
So don't feel like you need to answer immediately if it's not your working hours, okay?
Okay, sure.
And this is usually the way that I sort of work, or have been working in the past as well.
Don't worry about the time.
Just send me a message whenever.
If it's outside my work hours, I will not get the notification.
And once I'm logged back in, I'll get the notification.
Okay, same for me.
That's great.
And when do you think it will be a good time for us to generate that first assignment for this client?
To be honest, it's a little difficult to pin that down because I don't even have access to the tools yet.
Like I don't know the tools, anything about the tools.
So I mean, yeah, I mean like the walkthrough is scheduled for this Friday for all the products and the tools.
So I think after that is a good timeline to set.
So maybe in the middle of next week, what do you say?
Yeah.
Thank you.
Of course, counting on those that grant you access to everything you need.
I wanted to give practical stuff to work because I think that's the best way to learn, but I also want to be respectful of your onboarding schedule.
Yeah, absolutely.
I'll let you know if the tool watch-through happens before.
If it does, I can, of course, take it forward.
I'm definitely one of those people as well who believe that you just learn by doing things.
So there's definitely proof to that.
Once I have access, once I at least have even like a slight idea of what the product and what the tool actually is and how you're supposed to even log in and go about it, I think I can pick that up.
Yes, I feel like I'm jumping the gun.
Yeah, it's okay.
So, okay.
Yeah, wanted to introduce myself and like to tell you a little bit of how the work week look look like for us.
So we have that daily stand up.
There's the geek bot on Slack.
You saw that?
Yeah, you already replied to it, right?
Yes.
We have that.
Also, usually the work on Monday.
Monday is a super busy day because like we have to, this is my job to create a Monday update for Dave, for them to understand.
And we have a channel for the clients and we have the internal channel and the external that has the client in this channel.
So in the internal channel on Monday, I post an update about the account.
So show you read to for you to be aware of what's going on.
little bit.
So It's.daddy.
It's Vroom?
It's Thank Have
And usually on Mondays, Monday and Tuesday, I'll try to assign all the work for the week.
For example, for Imibeads, they have the editorial content work stream.
And that usually is around seven to nine articles a week that we need to deliver client.
So usually my goal is to have everything ready for you to work on during the week.
And your work is going to be generate the articles in the workflows.
But more than that, it's to really edit and deliver a version that is as close to what you consider a quality piece of content.
And to learn about the client as we go in the future, the idea for the work.

Workflows is to turn it into a platform that we could feed with intelligence about the clients, but we don't have that, so we have the LLMs connected to the workflows and everything else, so we have to be the ones building these and taking these notes, for example, if the client adds a comment more than once, like we have to be adding this knowledge to a base, and reflecting this information in the next article, so you have the week to generate this content.

Right now, we will be working with one client, but in the future, I don't know how it's going to be, but ideally, I know that the goal of companies to have one content manager for every client, so this is what we will be working for now.
Okay.
And, and, like, there's a lot of, um, .
Like, I'm getting that.
I don't know if you are experienced creating content with AI.
I don't know a lot about your background and stuff.
I'll be happy to cover it.
So I come with seven years of experience, particularly within B2B IT.
So I have worked with those companies before that specialize in energy solutions and, you know, more high-tech sort of stuff.
Also, in regards to particularly content, I should say most of my experience has been content-driven.

And in the last year, year and a half, I have done a lot of AI-generated content, both for, like, the written content as well as even for, like, videos and all of these things that you see that you just feed and then there's somebody speaking and then the way it happens and all of those things.
So, yeah, so, I have a little bit of experience with all of these.
That's great.
Because a lot of times AI will come up with...
Something that is not that great.
And sometimes we like some of it and another part we don't like it.
So it's up to us to read everything, to make sure.
Sometimes if you don't like the opening paragraph, we know that the goal of the first paragraph is to make the person want to read the next paragraph.
And so it goes.
So we have to be adapting this content, editing it.

Sometimes the text can get a little bit too formulaic, like use the same structures and we have to be improving that, some of it manually, but also making sure we are suggesting the improvements we need for the workflows.
And you will be the person identifying these needs.
Yeah.
So turning these into tickets, let's say you're running into an error or let's say if the workflow is...

Adding wrong links that are 404s, so you have to be the one flagging that, maybe in the client internal channel, and then we have a project management tool that is linear, where you can turn a message, you're just turning to linear tickets.
And so we have to be also, so one question I had when I started, like, do I have to, do I need to take, like, this, or can I also use Chat2PT, like I was doing prior to working?
Yes, a lot of the times you will rewrite whole paragraphs and sometimes iterate a lot, like improve this outline and then go back to the workflow.
There's a lot of that.
Sorry, I feel like I'm talking too much.
I just wanted to know how it would be.

And we have also a weekly meeting with the client, where we discuss the work that's ongoing, and you don't need to participate if you have too much focus work to do, you can skip the client meetings, but the meetings are an opportunity for us to ask questions.
For example, I have another client, which is the Adventurers Group.
They sell tours in Mexico, and they have 120 tours.
And so if I have questions, is this beach close to this other beach or stuff like that?
So I take time to ask those questions.
And these answers are really relevant.
And ideally, we would be adding this information to that intelligence or notion that we're building around the client.
So...
Thank
Ideas that we're turning people really experts around the client solutions and their branding, their voice, their funnels, like reflect about the articles we're working on.
So we have AI ways to, AI is how we do it, what we do it is really good content.

So it's really important that, let's say, you have a task to create an article around top 20 things to do in Cancun with kids, that you take the time to type that search, like a step into the persona's shoes and try to replicate that moment to reflect about the article.
Like, is it a good title?
Could I add more keywords?
Because like the do the keyword research based on.
That's the main keyword.
But we can always go to SEMrush.
So if I'm writing for the top 20 things to do in Cancun with kids, can also things to do with children.
I can also add that.
That's kind of rational.
We do it in an article by article basis.
Yeah, so I think I wanted to give you this outline for you to have a better understanding.
I remember that when I started, I didn't have a lot of clarity, like what am I supposed to do?
So I want to give you that a little bit more context.
No, this is extremely helpful.
Thank you so much.
There were definitely a lot of gaps in terms of what I'm supposed to go through, how am I supposed to, how is my day supposed to look like, and all of those things.
And I was still figuring that out, but it just has massive space.
Yeah.
Okay, I want to, I'll send you the link to the, that Immobit.
I am a space on Notion for you, but take your time, like if you find like half an hour in between your onboarding, you could take a look.
Yeah, absolutely.
I can definitely start at least looking them up, seeing what they have, the type of content that already exists.
Just check their personas and all of those things.
And then once I get access to the tools and all, I think all of this time spent on research will just make things easier then.
Yes.
And I'll send you the link also.
I probably don't have access to that.
It's Fathom.
Fathom is a tool.
It's really great.
You're using it?
Are you using it already?
I am.
It asks if I wanted it for every meeting or if I wanted it for specific meetings.
At the moment, I've set it for specific meetings.
I just ask before meeting if I want to be recording and taking notes.
Because I've noticed that and I recall that at least like, for you're If want from
It's 10 different note takers.

Yeah, anyway, I use that a lot, for example, especially there's one tab that is Ask Fathom, and then I can go there and say, what did Simran say about the status of the programmatic pages, for example?
And then you will, like, transcript your answer and then point the exact point of the conversation where you talk about it.
So I really use that resource.
And so the link to the kickoff meeting with Immobid, okay?
So watch it.
Okay, so thanks.
Welcome to the team again.
If you have any questions, feel free to reach out.
Thank you so much.
a very good day.
I'll see you then.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce Simran to the team and provide context on workflows, client projects, and expectations.](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Simran joins GrowthX as a content manager, with Immubit as her first client project](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=45.0)
  - [Mariana offers support for onboarding, emphasizing learning through practical work](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [Weekly workflow: Monday updates, content assignment early week, 7-9 articles/week delivery](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)
  - [AI-assisted content creation with focus on quality, editing, and continuous improvement](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=837.0)

## Topics

### Onboarding and Tool Access

  - [Simran in day 2 of onboarding, awaiting access to tools](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=506.0)
  - [Tool walkthrough scheduled for Friday](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
  - [Weis (W-E-I-E-S) handles access to all systems](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=347.0)
  - [Bridget manages onboarding docs; Simran to ask her about ETD delivery and onboarding teams access](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)

### Client Project: Immubit

  - [AI solution optimizing energy consumption in factory plants](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=145.0)
  - [Niche keywords, less competition](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=175.0)
  - [Kickoff meeting last week; strategy and first articles to be built together](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Mariana to share Notion doc with client info](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=182.0)
  - [Tentative timeline: mid-next week for first assignment (post-tool walkthrough)](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=533.0)

### Work Schedule and Communication

  - [Simran to add working hours to calendar](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
  - [Async communication encouraged; no expectation of immediate responses outside work hours](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=431.0)
  - [Daily standup via Geekbot on Slack](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=613.0)

### Content Creation Workflow

  - [Monday/Tuesday: Mariana assigns work for the week](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Content managers edit AI-generated content for quality and client-specific knowledge](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=837.0)
  - [Goal: 7-9 articles/week for Immubit's editorial content workstream](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=673.0)
  - [Emphasis on continuous improvement of AI outputs and workflows](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)

### AI and Content Management

  - [Simran has 7 years of B2B IT experience, including AI-generated content](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=794.0)
  - [Focus on editing, improving AI outputs, and adapting to client needs](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=837.0)
  - [Encouraged to use additional tools (e.g., ChatGPT) for content improvement](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=942.0)

### Client Interactions

  - [Weekly client meetings (optional for content managers)](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
  - [Opportunity to ask questions and gather client-specific information](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=987.0)
  - [Building comprehensive client knowledge base](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1007.0)

### SEO and Research

  - [Keyword research using tools like SEMrush](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1075.0)
  - [Emphasis on understanding user intent and expanding keyword opportunities](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1047.0)

### Tools and Resources

  - [Fathom for meeting transcripts and easy reference](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1202.0)
  - [Linear for project management and ticket creation](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=892.0)
  - [Notion for client information and knowledge base](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=336.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Simran to review Immubit Notion space when time allows](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [Mariana to send link to Immubit kickoff meeting recording](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1236.0)
  - [Simran to complete scheduled onboarding tasks and tool walkthrough](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
  - [Plan for first Immubit assignment mid-next week](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=533.0)
  - [Simran to familiarize herself with Fathom, Linear, and other tools as access is granted](https://fathom.video/share/RqB18VbwAZs-Jt2teyRQGAsYxFR9sz3T?tab=summary&timestamp=1184.0)

action_items.description: Send Simran document with info about Immubit client
Send Simran link to Immubit space on Notion
Review Immubit client info when received from Mariana
Share Fathom link of Immubit kickoff meeting recording with Simran
Watch Immubit kickoff meeting recording when link received
